model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Louisville holds a commanding ~9.65-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over South Florida (KenPom #18, AdjEM +25.42 vs. KenPom #50, AdjEM +15.77), with both their offense and defense ranking in the top 23 nationally — a level of two-way excellence that South Florida's AAC-tested profile cannot match. The Cardinals opened as 6.5-point favorites, carry ACC battle-hardening from a 23-10 season, and received a positive injury update with freshman guard Mikel Brown Jr. targeted to return at full strength after missing the ACC Tournament. South Florida was the #1 seed in the American Athletic Conference with a solid 24-8 record, but Tournament Index grades them as a below-average 11-seed (23rd of 52 over 13 tournaments, projecting fewer than 0.4 wins), undercutting their upset credentials despite the historically moderate 11-over-6 upset rate.

reasoningStep.title: Louisville vs. South Florida — Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Louisville holds a clear and consistent edge across every major metric: efficiency ratings, schedule strength, seeding, and betting market. South Florida is a legitimate mid-major program but is graded as a below-average 11-seed, and the ~9.65-point AdjEM gap is too large to overcome without a significant performance outlier. The Mikel Brown Jr. injury concern appears resolved, further solidifying Louisville's case.
evidence: KenPom: Louisville #18 (AdjEM +25.42, AdjO 124.1 rank 20, AdjD 98.7) vs. South Florida #50 (AdjEM +15.77, AdjO 117.7 rank 58, AdjD 101.9) — ~9.65-point efficiency gap
Torvik: Louisville #16 (AdjOE 124.0 rank 23, AdjDE 98.1 rank 23) vs. South Florida #50 (AdjOE 117.2 rank 65, AdjDE 102.3 rank 60)
Betting market: Louisville opened as a 6.5-point favorite over South Florida (O/U 163.5) per Action Network
Seeding and conference: Louisville is the ACC #6 seed (23-10); South Florida is the AAC #11 seed (24-8, AAC #1 seed)
Tournament Index grades South Florida as 23rd-strongest of 52 No. 11 seeds over last 13 tournaments, projecting only 0.37 average wins — a below-average 11-seed profile
Injury update: Mikel Brown Jr. missed the ACC Tournament but Pat Kelsey confirmed the goal was to have him 100% for the NCAA Tournament first round

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

result.snippet: Tournament Format & Schedule The 2026 American Conference Men's Basketball Championship features a 10-team, five-day stepladder bracket. As the No. 1 seed , South Florida receives a double-bye directly to Saturday's semifinals at Legacy Arena at the BJCC.

result.title: South Florida March Madness opponent is Louisville in 2026 NCAA bracket

result.snippet: Tournament Index evaluation South Florida earned a No. 11 seed at the NCAA Tournament . In terms of seed strength, the Tournament Index grades the Bulls as the 23rd-strongest No. 11 seed of the last 13 tournaments (out of 52 R64 teams). For South Florida , the TI projects an average of 0.37 wins given its seed and team strength.

result.title: American Conference Men's Basketball Championship Tournament Bracket ...
result.snippet: IRVING, Texas - The field is set for the 2026 American Conference Men's Basketball Championship, with South Florida earning the No. 1 seed . The championship will take place March 11 -15 at Legacy Arena at the BJCC in Birmingham, Alabama.

The Cardinals opened as 6.5-point favorites, carry ACC battle-hardening from a 23-10 season, and received a positive injury update with freshman guard Mikel Brown Jr. targeted to return at full strength after missing the ACC Tournament. South Florida was the #1 seed in the American Athletic Conference with a solid 24-8 record, but Tournament Index grades them as a below-average 11-seed (23rd of 52 over 13 tournaments, projecting fewer than 0.4 wins), undercutting their upset credentials despite the historically moderate 11-over-6 upset rate."},"reasoningStep":{"id":"r64-east-5-analysis","title":"Louisville vs. South Florida — Round of 64 Analysis","summary":"Louisville holds a clear and consistent edge across every major metric: efficiency ratings, schedule strength, seeding, and betting market.
